docs.title: Multimodal Agent
docs.text:
A clean, modern multimodal AI agent powered by Google Gemini, built by Horam.

This library provides:

  • Text generation
  • Image + text multimodal prompts
  • Interactive chat mode
  • Local conversation memory
  • Retry logic with exponential backoff
  • Custom exceptions for safe error handling
  • Structured CLI (agent)
  • Full logging utilities
  • 85%+ test coverage
  • PyPI-ready project structure

docs.title: What’s New (v0.2.0)
docs.text:
• Local memory system
  • agent history show | delete | reset | summary
  • Improved chat mode error handling
  • More modular CLI
  • Updated tests & fixtures
  • Cleaner logging for CLI mode

docs.title: Why this project exists
docs.text:
The official Gemini examples are minimal. This package demonstrates how to build a production-quality, testable, extensible AI agent with:

  • Proper package architecture
  • Retry + failure handling
  • Custom exceptions
  • CLI tooling
  • Strong test coverage
  • Professional project layout

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Installation
  • Quickstart
  • CLI Usage

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
multimodal_agent/
├── agent_core.py
├── cli.py
├── utils.py
├── logger.py
├── errors.py
└── tests/

docs.title: Features at a Glance
docs.text:
✔ Gemini API wrapper

✔ Multimodal (text + image)

✔ Retry with exponential backoff

✔ Model overload protection

✔ Custom image loader with MIME detection

✔ CLI tool (agent)

✔ Custom structured logs

✔ Fully tested (85%+)

✔ PyPI-ready packaging

docs.title: The Agent Class
docs.text:
multimodal_agent.agent_core.MultiModalAgent

The main class powering multimodal communication with Gemini.

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent(model="gemini-2.5-flash")
Arguments: | Arg | Type | Default | Description | | ---- | ---- | ------- | ----------------- | | model | str | "gemini-2.5-flash" | Gemini model name | | api_version | str | "v1" | Gemini API version | | client | genai.Client | None | Inject custom client (used for testing)|

docs.title: ask(prompt: str) -> str
docs.text:
Sends a text-only request.

agent.ask("What is recursion?")

docs.title: ask_with_image(prompt: str, image: Part) -> str
docs.text:
Sends an image + text multimodal prompt.

image = load_image_as_part("photo.jpg")
agent.ask_with_image("Describe this image.", image)

docs.title: chat()
docs.text:
Starts a REPL (interactive loop) with memory persistence.

agent chat

docs.title: Retry Logic
docs.text:
All request methods use:

safe_generate_content()

Which includes:

  • Exponential backoff

  • Retryable errors (503 overload)

  • RetryableError and NonRetryableError exceptions

docs.title: Exceptions Raised
docs.text:
• RetryableError

  • NonRetryableError

  • AgentError (base class)

docs.title: CLI Usage
docs.text: The project installs an executable named: agent

docs.title: Show version
docs.text: agent --version

docs.title: Ask a Text Question
docs.text: agent ask "Explain quantum entanglement.

docs.title: Describe an Image
docs.text: agent image photo.jpg "what is in this image?"

docs.title: Chat Mode (with memory)
docs.text: agent chat

docs.title: Debug Mode
docs.text: agent --debug ask "hello"

docs.title: Change Model
docs.text: agent --model gemini-pro ask "explain transformers"

docs.title: Help
docs.text:
agent --help
Shows all subcommands and usage examples.

docs.title: Show history
docs.text: agent history show

docs.title: Delete one entry
docs.text: agent history delete 2

docs.title: Reset history
docs.text: agent history reset

docs.title: Summarize conversation
docs.text: agent history summary

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: The agent defines structured custom exceptions:

docs.title: AgentError
docs.text: Base class for all custom agent exceptions.

docs.title: RetryableError
docs.text:
Raised when:

  • Gemini returns HTTP 503
  • Model is overloaded
  • Retries are exhausted
  • Exponential backoff completes without success

docs.title: NonRetryableError
docs.text:
Raised when:

  • The model throws a non-retryable exception
  • Unexpected errors occur in the API call

docs.title: InvalidImageError
docs.text:
Raised by:

  • load_image_as_part()
  • Bad paths
  • Unsupported formats
  • PIL decoding failures 
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part
from multimodal_agent.errors import InvalidImageError

try:
    img = load_image_as_part("bad.png")
except InvalidImageError as e:
    print("Could not load image:", e)

docs.title: Simple Text Request
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent()
print(agent.ask("hello"))

docs.title: Custom Model
docs.text: agent --model gemini-pro ask "summarize transformers"

docs.title: Multimodal Request
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent()
img = load_image_as_part("cat.jpg")

print(agent.ask_with_image("describe this", img))

docs.title: Error Handling Example
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent
from multimodal_agent.errors import AgentError

agent = MultiModalAgent()

try:
    print(agent.ask("hello"))
except AgentError as e:
    print("The request failed:", e)

docs.title: Install from PyPI (recommended)
docs.text: pip install multimodal-agent

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/horam/multimodal-agent.git
cd multimodal-agent
pip install -e .[dev,test]

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the project root:

GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_api_key
LOGLEVEL=INFO
The CLI automatically loads:

ROOT/.env
This file is intentionally excluded by .gitignore.

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text:
agent --version
If the command runs successfully, the installation is complete.

docs.title: Logging
docs.text: The package uses a unified logging utility:

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Consistent formatting
  • Output to stdout
  • Prevents duplicate handlers
  • Respects LOGLEVEL environment variable
  • Works with pytest caplog

docs.title: Changing Log Level
docs.text:
In .env:

LOGLEVEL=DEBUG

Or via CLI:

agent --debug ask "hello"

docs.title: Memory System
docs.text:
The agent stores conversation turns in:

~/.multimodal_agent/history.json

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
• load_memory()

  • save_memory(list)

  • append_memory(entry)

  • delete_memory_index(i)

  • reset_memory()

  • summarize_memory()

Memory is plain JSON — easy to inspect and portable.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Below are the minimum examples needed to use the agent.

docs.title: Text Generation
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent()
response = agent.ask("Tell me something interesting about the universe.")
print(response)

docs.title: Image + Text Generation
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part

agent = MultiModalAgent()

image = load_image_as_part("cat.jpg")
response = agent.ask_with_image("Describe this cat.", image)

print(response)

docs.title: CLI Quickstart
docs.text:
agent ask "hello world"
agent image photo.jpg "what do you see?"
agent chat
agent --version
agent --debug ask "hello"

docs.title: Error Handling Example
docs.text:
Copy code
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent
from multimodal_agent.errors import AgentError

agent = MultiModalAgent()

try:
    print(agent.ask("hello"))
except AgentError as e:
    print("The request failed:", e)
